themes: agent-native PM role & team composition math
model competition (Kimi K2.6, GLM 5.1, Gemini perception gap)
utility vs. delight gap in AI products

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Official Claude Code post-mortem thread: three confirmed bugs fixed in v2.1.116+, root cause was Agent SDK harness not model regression — operationally important for anyone building on Claude Code.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Box's CTO reports a 10-point accuracy jump on enterprise content tasks with GPT-5.5 vs GPT-5.4 — concrete enterprise eval data, not hype.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Frames Anthropic's silence as a reverse Streisand effect actively fueling user defection as GPT-5.5 lands — useful strategic read on competitive momentum.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Reframes 'PMs should code' as a product intuition story, not a programming skill story — directly relevant to agent-native PM role thesis.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Makes the team composition math explicit: a PM who ships with Claude Code changes the 5-eng+1-PM headcount model, which is the real disruption story.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: 648K-view post on Gemini's coding perception gap is a genuine data point about where enterprise switching risk is and isn't coming from.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Crisp argument that utility alone doesn't create pull and most AI products are failing the delight test — directly useful for AI product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Asana giving Claude agents their own user accounts is a concrete example of how enterprise agent adoption is actually happening inside real PM workflows.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's prompt injection at country scale framing is a real product risk signal for anyone building agent-to-production systems.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Cortex-AI shared memory layer across Claude Code, Windsurf, and ChatGPT is a practical tool for indie devs running multi-tool agent workflows.

voices.why: Direct source on Claude Code internals, post-mortems, and product changes — essential for anyone building on the platform.
Sharp takes on AI product strategy and distribution that are original and high-signal, not repackaged takes.
Consistent output on agent-native PM role and team composition math, relevant to operator's core interests — though high noise ratio in this feed.
Useful bellwether for power-user sentiment on model quality and Anthropic competitive positioning.
Enterprise practitioner with real eval data on model performance, not just vibes.

examples: RoundtableSpace all-caps hype posts with no original analysis (trading tools, lock-in mantras, crypto sponsorships)
aakashgupta off-topic viral content (cocaine in Thames, Down syndrome genetics, Ratatouille game history, protein shakes)
JaynitMakwana thread spam promoting Cappy video editor with near-zero engagement

emerging.theme: agent-native PM role & team composition math
